campuses.name: Downtown Campus
campuses.description: Our central hub in the heart of downtown, hosting worship services, outreach programs, and weekday workshops including financial coaching classes.

campuses.name: Midtown Campus
campuses.description: A vibrant campus near transit and universities, focused on young adult ministry and community service projects.

campuses.name: Westside Campus
campuses.description: Located in Atlanta’s Westside neighborhood, this campus hosts regular neighborhood cleanups and food distribution events.

campuses.name: Eastside Campus
campuses.description: Serving the East Atlanta community with family-oriented ministries, youth outreach, and a weekly food pantry.

campuses.name: Cleveland Campus
campuses.description: Our Cleveland campus supports neighborhood cleanups, outreach events, and partnerships with local food resources.

food_resources.name: Grace Downtown Food Pantry
food_resources.description: Church-based food pantry serving low-income residents near downtown Atlanta with a mix of shelf-stable items and fresh foods.
food_resources.notes: Open Wednesdays 3:00–6:00 PM and Saturdays 10:00 AM–12:00 PM. Fresh produce available most weeks.

food_resources.name: Midtown Community Meals
food_resources.description: Free hot dinner program serving anyone in need, located near Midtown Campus.
food_resources.notes: Community dinner served Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays 5:30–7:00 PM.

food_resources.name: Westside Family Pantry
food_resources.description: Neighborhood pantry focusing on families with children, offering groceries and basic household items.
food_resources.notes: Open Tuesdays and Thursdays 10:00 AM–2:00 PM. Limited fresh produce available on Thursdays.

food_resources.name: Eastside Fresh Market Pantry
food_resources.description: Choice-style pantry emphasizing fresh fruits and vegetables alongside shelf-stable items.
food_resources.notes: Open Wednesdays 4:00–7:00 PM and Fridays 1:00–4:00 PM. Fresh produce and some refrigerated items available.

food_resources.name: Downtown Community Fridge
food_resources.description: Outdoor fridge and pantry with free, take-what-you-need food items, restocked daily by volunteers.
food_resources.notes: Outdoor fridge accessible daily 7:00 AM–9:00 PM. Stock varies.

food_resources.name: Atlanta Mobile Meal Truck
food_resources.description: Mobile outreach providing hot meals at multiple stops across the city.
food_resources.notes: Check website for current weekly route. Stops often include downtown and Westside neighborhoods.

food_resources.name: Cleveland Neighborhood Pantry
food_resources.description: Community-run pantry near our Cleveland Campus offering groceries to local residents.
food_resources.notes: Open Wednesdays 1:00–5:00 PM and Saturdays 9:00 AM–12:00 PM. Partner site for neighborhood cleanups.

food_resources.name: Cleveland Family Meal Center
food_resources.description: Indoor meal program serving free lunches and dinners for families and seniors.
food_resources.notes: Lunch served daily at 12:00 PM, dinner Monday–Friday at 5:30 PM.

food_resources.name: Old Fourth Ward Pantry
food_resources.description: Volunteer-led pantry offering nonperishable food and occasional produce boxes.
food_resources.notes: Open Wednesdays 10:00 AM–1:00 PM and the first Saturday of the month.

food_resources.name: South Atlanta Faith Pantry
food_resources.description: Local church pantry providing groceries and prayer support to neighbors.
food_resources.notes: Open Wednesdays 2:00–6:00 PM and Fridays 10:00 AM–1:00 PM. Fresh produce typically available after 3:00 PM on Wednesdays.

food_resources.name: Edgewood Community Fridge
food_resources.description: 24/7 fridge and pantry space at a neighborhood storefront.
food_resources.notes: Open 24 hours. Donations accepted directly at the fridge.

food_resources.name: Hunger Relief Resource Center
food_resources.description: Regional hub connecting residents to multiple food programs, SNAP assistance, and nutrition education.

food_resources.name: Near East Side Food Shelf
food_resources.description: Small neighborhood food shelf serving the near east side of Cleveland.
food_resources.notes: Open Tuesdays 10:00 AM–1:00 PM and Thursdays 3:00–6:00 PM.

food_resources.name: Central City Senior Groceries
food_resources.description: Grocery box distribution program dedicated to low-income seniors in downtown Atlanta.
food_resources.notes: Monthly distribution on the second Wednesday, 2:00–4:00 PM. Pre-registration required.

food_resources.name: Hope in the Hills Food Pantry
food_resources.description: Faith-based pantry serving families with regular evening hours.
food_resources.address_line1: 3400 Martin Luther King Jr Dr SW
food_resources.notes: Open Wednesdays 3:30–7:00 PM and Fridays 11:00 AM–2:00 PM. Often has fresh produce and bread available after 3:00 PM on Wednesdays.

funds.name: Youth Outreach
funds.description: Supports after-school programs, mentoring, and outreach events for students and young adults.

funds.name: Local Food Pantry
funds.description: Provides ongoing support for our partner food pantries and community fridges.

funds.name: Community & Neighborhood Projects
funds.description: Funds neighborhood cleanups, block parties, and local improvement efforts.

funds.name: Global Missions
funds.description: Supports international partners providing relief, church planting, and community development.

funds.name: Benevolence Fund
funds.description: Provides emergency financial assistance to individuals and families in crisis.

events.title: Financial Coaching Workshop: Budget Basics
events.description: A practical, free in-person workshop to help you create a realistic budget, understand your cash flow, and set short-term financial goals.

events.title: Financial Coaching Workshop: Debt & Savings Strategy
events.description: Learn how to prioritize debts, build an emergency fund, and set up sustainable savings habits.

events.title: Job Readiness Class: Resume & Interview Skills
events.description: Interactive class to strengthen your resume, cover letter, and interview confidence.

events.title: Parenting Class: Faith & Family Routines
events.description: A four-week class helping parents build healthy rhythms of faith, communication, and boundaries at home.

events.title: Serve the City: Downtown Street Outreach
events.description: Join our outreach team to distribute care packs, listen, and pray with neighbors experiencing homelessness downtown.

events.title: Eastside Community Dinner & Connection Night
events.description: Evening outreach event with a free community meal, conversation tables, and prayer teams.

events.title: Westside Neighborhood Block Party & Resource Fair
events.description: Evening block party with food, games, and community resource tables in partnership with local organizations.

events.title: Cleveland Downtown Neighborhood Cleanup
events.description: Help pick up litter and beautify streets around the Cleveland Campus. All supplies provided; light activity suitable for most ages.

events.title: Cleveland Near East Park Cleanup
events.description: Join neighbors to clean up a local park and greenway on Cleveland’s near east side.

events.title: Westside BeltLine Neighborhood Cleanup
events.description: Light neighborhood cleanup along a section of the BeltLine near Westside Campus.

events.title: Sunday Worship Gathering - Downtown
events.description: Weekly worship service with teaching, music, and prayer.

events.title: Grief & Loss Support Group
events.description: A safe, faith-based group for those grieving the loss of a loved one.

events.title: Financial Stewardship Webinar: Planning for the Future
events.description: Online webinar covering long-term financial planning, giving, and wise stewardship.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Downtown Evening Food Pantry Team
volunteer_opportunities.description: Serve guests at the Grace Downtown Food Pantry by greeting, stocking shelves, and helping with check-in during evening hours.
volunteer_opportunities.requirements: Ages 16+. Light lifting up to 20 lbs. Brief orientation required before first shift.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Midtown Pantry Packing & Sorting
volunteer_opportunities.description: Help organize donations, pack grocery bags, and prepare for meal program nights at Midtown Community Meals.
volunteer_opportunities.requirements: Comfortable standing for up to 2 hours. Great for small groups or individuals.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Eastside Fresh Market Pantry Volunteers
volunteer_opportunities.description: Assist guests in choosing groceries, restock produce tables, and offer a welcoming presence at the Eastside Fresh Market Pantry.
volunteer_opportunities.requirements: Spanish speakers especially welcome. Orientation and background check for ongoing volunteers.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Westside Family Pantry Volunteer Team
volunteer_opportunities.description: Serve families at the Westside Family Pantry by packing boxes, loading cars, and offering prayer when requested.
volunteer_opportunities.requirements: Ability to lift up to 30 lbs helpful but not required. Family-friendly opportunity.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Cleveland Neighborhood Pantry Team
volunteer_opportunities.description: Help neighbors select groceries, stock shelves, and keep the Cleveland Neighborhood Pantry clean and welcoming.
volunteer_opportunities.requirements: Training provided on-site. Volunteers under 16 must serve with a parent or guardian.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Cleveland Downtown Light-Activity Cleanup Crew
volunteer_opportunities.description: Join a light-activity crew to pick up litter and sweep sidewalks around the Cleveland Campus and nearby blocks.
volunteer_opportunities.requirements: Wear closed-toe shoes. All cleanup supplies provided.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Westside Neighborhood Cleanup Crew
volunteer_opportunities.description: Support regular neighborhood cleanups in the Westside community, focusing on alleyways and common areas.
volunteer_opportunities.requirements: Comfortable walking outdoors for up to 2 hours. Gloves provided.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Midtown After-School Tutoring
volunteer_opportunities.description: Provide homework help and encouragement to elementary and middle school students one afternoon per week.
volunteer_opportunities.requirements: Background check required. Training session before serving.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Downtown Campus Admin Support
volunteer_opportunities.description: Help with data entry, mailings, and reception coverage at the Downtown Campus office.
volunteer_opportunities.requirements: Comfort with basic computer tasks. Weekday daytime availability preferred.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Eastside Wednesday Night Childcare Team
volunteer_opportunities.description: Provide safe, fun childcare during midweek classes and support groups at the Eastside Campus.
volunteer_opportunities.requirements: Background check and childcare orientation required. Minimum age 18.
